components.role: Dual op-amp — provides active bass and treble shelving EQ with boost and cut capability
Bass control pot — center detent is flat; CW boosts bass, CCW cuts bass
Treble control pot — center detent is flat; CW boosts treble, CCW cuts treble
Bass frequency-setting capacitor — determines the bass shelf transition frequency
Treble frequency-setting capacitor — determines the treble shelf transition frequency
Input resistor — sets flat-band gain and input impedance
Feedback resistor — sets flat-band gain (unity when Rf = Rin)

dsp_model.description: Two independent first-order shelf filters — bass shelf and treble shelf — each with boost/cut capability

behavioral_description: The Baxandall tone control is the gold standard for active bass/treble EQ in pedals and hi-fi equipment. It provides independent, symmetrical boost and cut of bass and treble frequencies with a smooth, musical response. At center position (pots at noon), the response is flat with unity gain. The bass and treble controls are largely independent — adjusting one has minimal effect on the other — which makes it intuitive to use. The active topology means boost and cut are available in equal amounts (typically +/-15dB), unlike passive tone stacks that can only cut. The interaction between the two shelves creates a natural, pleasing mid-scoop or mid-hump depending on settings, making it easy to sculpt a wide range of tones from scooped metal to fat bluesy warmth.
